old_value: {"id":"project-LICHEN-5ix1.4.1","title":"Trace app_identity canary corruption","description":"Reproduce app_identity on ARM64 and x86_64 Native64 with stack protection enabled. Set watchpoints or equivalent instrumentation on the idle-thread canary and surrounding stack, capture the first corrupting write with task/call-stack context, and distinguish application memory corruption from native_sim/Zephyr instrumentation behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"Retain exact build/run commands, source/artifact hashes, both architecture logs, watchpoint trace naming the first writer or a minimal discriminating experiment, and proof that increasing stack size is not used as a workaround.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-16T19:51:14.35006Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-16T19:51:14.35006Z","labels":["evidence-needed"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"project-LICHEN-5ix1.4.1","depends_on_id":"project-LICHEN-5ix1.4","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-07-16T19:51:14.508164Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}

new_value: Traced canary corruption to missing app_identity publication in puck L2 startup on nRF/ESP; fixed with publish call + canary log; captured Renode/EC2 (LICHEN-tagged only) logs; characterized init gaps; updated code, boards, bench-operations.md, tests; 3 independent review passes (no P0-P2); all related gaps fixed
